devanagari: कोपान् मुसलधारावर्षं वर्षतीन्द्रे व्रजौकसां रक्षणार्थं गोवर्धनधारणम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । इन्द्रस्तदाऽत्मनः पूजां विज्ञाय विहतां नृप । गोपे...
english_devnagari: kopān musaladhārāvarṣaṃ varṣatīndre vrajaukasāṃ rakṣaṇārthaṃ govardhanadhāraṇamśrīśuka uvācaindrastadā'tmanaḥ pūjāṃ vijñāya vihatāṃ nṛpagopebhyaḥ kṛṣṇanāthebhyo nandādibhyaścukopa ha
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Then perceiving that his worship had been stopped, Indra got enraged upon the cowherds headed by Nanda who regarded Kṛṣṇa as their Protector.

devanagari: गणं सांवर्तकं नाम मेघानां चान्तकारिणाम् । इन्द्रः प्रचोदयत् क्रुद्धो वाक्यं चाहेशमान्युत ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: gaṇaṃ sāṃvartakaṃ nāma meghānāṃ cāntakāriṇāmindraḥ pracodayat kruddho vākyaṃ cāheśamānyuta
translation: Being incensed, Indra directed the host of clouds called Saṃvartaka which bring about the destruction of the universe. Priding himself as the Supreme Ruler of the Universe he commanded:

devanagari: अहो श्रीमदमाहात्म्यं गोपानां काननौकसाम् । कृष्णं मर्त्यमुपाश्रित्य ये चक्रुर्देवहेलनम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: aho śrīmadamāhātmyaṃ gopānāṃ kānanaukasāmkṛṣṇaṃ martyamupāśritya ye cakrurdevahelanam
translation: “Oh! Look at the astounding audacity and arrogance due to wealth and prosperity of those cowherds who are after all forest-dwellers, but depending on a mortal (called) Kṛṣṇa, have set at nought a deity.

devanagari: यथादृढैः कर्ममयैः क्रतुभिर्नामनौनिभैः । विद्यां आन्वीक्षिकीं हित्वा तितीर्षन्ति भवार्णवम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yathādṛḍhaiḥ karmamayaiḥ kratubhirnāmanaunibhaiḥvidyāṃ ānvīkṣikīṃ hitvā titīrṣanti bhavārṇavam
translation: Giving up the path of knowledge involving the meditation of the Self or self-realization, they expect to cross the ocean of Saṃsāra or worldly existence with the help of the so-called unreliable boats in the form of sacrifices consisting of rituals.

devanagari: वाचालं बालिशं स्तब्धं अज्ञं पण्डितमानिनम् । कृष्णं मर्त्यमुपाश्रित्य गोपा मे चक्रुरप्रियम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vācālaṃ bāliśaṃ stabdhaṃ ajñaṃ paṇḍitamāninamkṛṣṇaṃ martyamupāśritya gopā me cakrurapriyam
translation: [1] Similarly, taking resort to that garrulous, foolish, arrogant, ignorant mortal Kṛṣṇa who (out of pedantry) regards himself as a great scholar, the cowherds have dared to offend me.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
VT.—tries to explain the implied glorification of Kṛṣṇa e.g. vācālam—whose word is effective or powerful. Paṇḍita-māninaṃ—respected by persons who are paṇḍitas or knowers of Brahman.
Padaratnāvalī Kṛṣṇa who is vācāla i.e. speaks what is censured by the scriptures and who regards himself as an authority (Panditamāninam).

devanagari: एषां श्रियावलिप्तानां कृष्णेनाध्मापितात्मनाम् । धुनुत श्रीमदस्तम्भं पशून् नयत सङ्क्षयम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣāṃ śriyāvaliptānāṃ kṛṣṇenādhmāpitātmanāmdhunuta śrīmadastambhaṃ paśūn nayata saṅkṣayam
translation: Shatter down (lit. shake off) the arrogance of affluence of the cowherds who are puffed with pride of wealth and prosperity and have their minds filled with insolence through the influence of Kṛṣṇa. Destroy all of their cattle.”

devanagari: अहं चैरावतं नागं आरुह्यानुव्रजे व्रजम् । मरुद्‍गणैर्महावेगैः नन्दगोष्ठजिघांसया ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ cairāvataṃ nāgaṃ āruhyānuvraje vrajammarud‍gaṇairmahāvegaiḥ nandagoṣṭhajighāṃsayā
translation: [2] With a view to annihilate the camp (the cattlewealth) of Nanda, I shall follow you riding my elephant Airāvata and accompanied with the troop of maruts of enormous prowess.”
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Cf. Viṣṇu Purāṇa. 5.11.5 and HV. 2.18.5

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं मघवताऽज्ञप्ता मेघा निर्मुक्तबन्धनाः । नन्दगोकुलमासारैः पीडयामासुरोजसा ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaitthaṃ maghavatā'jñaptā meghā nirmuktabandhanāḥnandagokulamāsāraiḥ pīḍayāmāsurojasā
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus commanded and freed (from their bonds) by Indra, the (saṃvarta) clouds vehemently harassed Nanda’s Gokula with heavy showers.

devanagari: विद्योतमाना विद्युद्भिः स्तनन्तः स्तनयित्‍नुभिः । तीव्रैर्मरुद्‍गणैर्नुन्ना ववृषुर्जलशर्कराः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyotamānā vidyudbhiḥ stanantaḥ stanayit‍nubhiḥtīvrairmarud‍gaṇairnunnā vavṛṣurjalaśarkarāḥ
translation: Dazzling with flashes of lightning and thundering with the roar of thunderbolts and impelled by tempestuous winds, they showered hail-stones.

devanagari: स्थूणास्थूला वर्षधारा मुञ्चत्स्वभ्रेष्वभीक्ष्णशः । जलौघैः प्लाव्यमाना भूः नादृश्यत नतोन्नतम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sthūṇāsthūlā varṣadhārā muñcatsvabhreṣvabhīkṣṇaśaḥjalaughaiḥ plāvyamānā bhūḥ nādṛśyata natonnatam
translation: When the clouds let loose incessant downpour of showers as big as columns (in volume), the earth was inundated with torrential floods of water, and the elevations and the depressions of the ground became invisible.

devanagari: अत्यासारातिवातेन पशवो जातवेपनाः । गोपा गोप्यश्च शीतार्ता गोविन्दं शरणं ययुः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: atyāsārātivātena paśavo jātavepanāḥgopā gopyaśca śītārtā govindaṃ śaraṇaṃ yayuḥ
translation: The cattle which were shivering (with cold) under excessively driving showers and tempestuous winds, and the cowherds and their women who were afflicted with severe cold, sought refuge in Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: शिरः सुतांश्च कायेन प्रच्छाद्या सारपीडिताः । वेपमाना भगवतः पादमूलमुपाययुः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śiraḥ sutāṃśca kāyena pracchādyā sārapīḍitāḥvepamānā bhagavataḥ pādamūlamupāyayuḥ
translation: Covering their heads and their children (and calves) with their bodies and acutely suffering from the torrential downpour, they approached shivering, to the feet of the Lord.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्ण महाभाग त्वन्नाथं गोकुलं प्रभो । त्रातुमर्हसि देवान्नः कुपिताद् भक्तवत्सल ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahābhāga tvannāthaṃ gokulaṃ prabhotrātumarhasi devānnaḥ kupitād bhaktavatsala
translation: “Oh Kṛṣṇa! Oh highly glorious Kṛṣṇa! The whole of Gokula has none else but you as its protector, Oh Lord! You are affectionate to your devotees. It behoves you to protect us from the enraged divinity (Indra)”.

devanagari: शिलावर्षानिपातेन हन्यमानमचेतनम् । निरीक्ष्य भगवान् मेने कुपितेन्द्रकृतं हरिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śilāvarṣānipātena hanyamānamacetanamnirīkṣya bhagavān mene kupitendrakṛtaṃ hariḥ
translation: Observing that due to the beating of the volley of the hail-stones, the Gokula had fallen senseless, the glorious Lord Hari came to know that it was the working of indignant Indra.

devanagari: अपर्त्त्वत्युल्बणं वर्षं अतिवातं शिलामयम् । स्वयागे विहतेऽस्माभिः इन्द्रो नाशाय वर्षति ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: aparttvatyulbaṇaṃ varṣaṃ ativātaṃ śilāmayamsvayāge vihate'smābhiḥ indro nāśāya varṣati
translation: “As the sacrifice to be offered to him has been obstructed by us, Indra is pouring down for our destruction tremendously heavy showers full of hail-stones, accompanied by terrific stormy winds, out of season.

devanagari: तत्र प्रतिविधिं सम्यग् आत्मयोगेन साधये । लोकेशमानिनां मौढ्याद् हनिष्ये श्रीमदं तमः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra pratividhiṃ samyag ātmayogena sādhayelokeśamānināṃ mauḍhyād haniṣye śrīmadaṃ tamaḥ
translation: Now I shall employ an effective counter-measure through my yogic power and remove the tamas (darkness) in the form of the arrogance of affluence of the divinities who, through their foolishness, regard themselves as the supreme ruler of the world.

devanagari: न हि सद्‍भावयुक्तानां सुराणामीशविस्मयः । मत्तोऽसतां मानभङ्गः प्रशमायोपकल्पते ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi sad‍bhāvayuktānāṃ surāṇāmīśavismayaḥmatto'satāṃ mānabhaṅgaḥ praśamāyopakalpate
translation: It is not desirable that celestials, who are endowed with the quality of Sattva (goodness) or devotion to me to a special degree, should be puffed up with the vain pride of being the (Supreme) ruler of the world. The humiliation of these wicked divinities at my hand will work as Grace unto them.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
(i) anugraha eva—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) Will lead to deeper devotion to me—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तस्मात् मच्छरणं गोष्ठं मन्नाथं मत्परिग्रहम् । गोपाये स्वात्मयोगेन सोऽयं मे व्रत आहितः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt maccharaṇaṃ goṣṭhaṃ mannāthaṃ matparigrahamgopāye svātmayogena so'yaṃ me vrata āhitaḥ
translation: With all the yogic power at my command, I will, therefore, protect this Gokula which has sought shelter in me and has accepted me as their Master and which now entirely depends on me. This is the eternal vow taken by me.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वैकेन हस्तेन कृत्वा गोवर्धनाचलम् । दधार लीलया कृष्णः छत्राकमिव बालकः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvaikena hastena kṛtvā govardhanācalamdadhāra līlayā kṛṣṇaḥ chatrākamiva bālakaḥ
translation: Observing thus (to himself), Kṛṣṇa, though a child, uprooted with ease the mount Govardhana withone of his hands[4] and held it up sportively as if it were a mushroom.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
with left hand—VT. He quotes HV 2.18.31.
sa dhṛtaḥ saṅgato meghair giriḥ savyena pāṇinā
VT.’s graphic description and dramatic narration of this episode deserves perusal in the original.

devanagari: अथाह भगवान् गोपान् हेऽम्ब तात व्रजौकसः । यथोपजोषं विशत गिरिगर्तं सगोधनाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: athāha bhagavān gopān he'mba tāta vrajaukasaḥyathopajoṣaṃ viśata girigartaṃ sagodhanāḥ
translation: And quoth the glorious Lord to cowherds “Oh mother! Oh father! Oh residents ofVraja. Enter comfortably into the cavity beneath the mountain along with your wealth of cattle.

devanagari: न त्रास इह वः कार्यो मद्धस्ताद्रिनिपातने । वातवर्षभयेनालं तत्त्राणं विहितं हि वः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: na trāsa iha vaḥ kāryo maddhastādrinipātanevātavarṣabhayenālaṃ tattrāṇaṃ vihitaṃ hi vaḥ
translation: You need not entertain any fear that the mountain will slip down from my hand. Dispel all the fear about storms or (driving) showers, for protection (against them) has been already securely devised.”

devanagari: तथा निर्विविशुर्गर्तं कृष्णाश्वासितमानसः । यथावकाशं सधनाः सव्रजाः सोपजीविनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā nirviviśurgartaṃ kṛṣṇāśvāsitamānasaḥyathāvakāśaṃ sadhanāḥ savrajāḥ sopajīvinaḥ
translation: With their minds thus reassured by Kṛṣṇa, they entered into the cavity (beneath Govardhana) along with their wealth, herds of cattle, their dependants (like priests etc.) food-grains etc. according to the accommodation (which was sufficiently available).

devanagari: क्षुत्तृड्व्यथां सुखापेक्षां हित्वा तैर्व्रजवासिभिः । वीक्ष्यमाणो दधावद्रिं सप्ताहं नाचलत् पदात् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣuttṛḍvyathāṃ sukhāpekṣāṃ hitvā tairvrajavāsibhiḥvīkṣyamāṇo dadhāvadriṃ saptāhaṃ nācalat padāt
translation: Being gazed at by those inhabitants of Vraja who disregarded their pangs of hunger and thirst and thus neglected their personal comforts, Kṛṣṇa up-held the mountain for seven days and did not move from his position.

devanagari: कृष्णयोगानुभावं तं निशम्येन्द्रोऽतिविस्मितः । निःस्तम्भो भ्रष्टसङ्कल्पः स्वान् मेघान् संन्यवारयत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇayogānubhāvaṃ taṃ niśamyendro'tivismitaḥniḥstambho bhraṣṭasaṅkalpaḥ svān meghān saṃnyavārayat
translation: Observing the yogic power of Kṛṣṇa, Indra was extremely astounded. Being shorn of pride and thwarted of his design, he restrained his saṃvartaka clouds.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे पञ्चविंशोऽध्यायः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe pañcaviṃśo'dhyāyaḥ
translation: Noticing the sky cleared of clouds, the sun already risen and the terrific tempestuous winds and the downpour of hails had ceased altogether, Kṛṣṇa, the bearer of Govardhana, told the cowherds.

devanagari: खं व्यभ्रमुदितादित्यं वातवर्षं च दारुणम् । निशम्योपरतं गोपान् गोवर्धनधरोऽब्रवीत् । निर्यात त्यजत त्रासं गोपाः सस्त्रीधनार्भकाः । उपारत...
english_devnagari: khaṃ vyabhramuditādityaṃ vātavarṣaṃ ca dāruṇamniśamyoparataṃ gopān govardhanadharo'bravītniryāta tyajata trāsaṃ gopāḥ sastrīdhanārbhakāḥupārataṃ vātavarṣaṃ vyudaprāyāśca nimnagāḥ
translation: “Oh cowherds! Now that the stormy winds and showers have stopped and the waters of the flooded rivers have almost receded, you dismiss all your apprehension and go out into the open with your women, children and property (cattle)

devanagari: ततस्ते निर्ययुर्गोपाः स्वं स्वमादाय गोधनम् । शकटोढोपकरणं स्त्रीबालस्थविराः शनैः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tataste niryayurgopāḥ svaṃ svamādāya godhanamśakaṭoḍhopakaraṇaṃ strībālasthavirāḥ śanaiḥ
translation: Taking with them their respective wealth (viz. herds of cattle) and all the cart-loads of their belongings, the cowherds slowly came out (of the cavity) along with their wives, children and elders.

devanagari: भगवानपि तं शैलं स्वस्थाने पूर्ववत्प्रभुः । पश्यतां सर्वभूतानां स्थापयामास लीलया ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānapi taṃ śailaṃ svasthāne pūrvavatprabhuḥpaśyatāṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ sthāpayāmāsa līlayā
translation: The glorious Lord on his part sportfully placed the mountain on its previous position while all beings stood a-gazing.

devanagari: तं प्रेमवेगान् निभृता व्रजौकसो । यथा समीयुः परिरम्भणादिभिः । गोप्यश्च सस्नेहमपूजयन् मुदा । दध्यक्षताद्भिर्युयुजुः सदाशिषः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ premavegān nibhṛtā vrajaukasoyathā samīyuḥ parirambhaṇādibhiḥgopyaśca sasnehamapūjayan mudādadhyakṣatādbhiryuyujuḥ sadāśiṣaḥ
translation: Overflowing with a flood of affection the inhabitants of Vraja approached him embracing (blessing, smelling the crown of his head and doing such acts expressive of love), according to their position and relations. The cowherd women overwhelmed with affection and joy worshipped him with curds and unbroken rice (akṣatas)[5] and expressed their choicest blessings profusely (out of grateful affection and not out of a sense of seniority as they knew his divine nature).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Subodhinī: The cowherd women made a circular mark of curds on his forehead on which some unbroken rice were applied and water was waved round his face and sipped.

devanagari: यशोदा रोहिणी नन्दो रामश्च बलिनां वरः । कृष्णमालिङ्ग्य युयुजुः आशिषः स्नेहकातराः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yaśodā rohiṇī nando rāmaśca balināṃ varaḥkṛṣṇamāliṅgya yuyujuḥ āśiṣaḥ snehakātarāḥ
translation: Yaśodā, Rohiṇī, Nanda and Balarāma, the mighty among the mightiest, were overwhelmed with affection and impatience and embracing Kṛṣṇa, they showered him with benedictions.

devanagari: दिवि देवगणाः सिद्धाः साध्या गन्धर्वचारणाः । तुष्टुवुर्मुमुचुस्तुष्टाः पुष्पवर्षाणि पार्थिव ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: divi devagaṇāḥ siddhāḥ sādhyā gandharvacāraṇāḥtuṣṭuvurmumucustuṣṭāḥ puṣpavarṣāṇi pārthiva
translation: In the heavens, hosts of celestials, Sādhyas, Siddhas, Gandharvas and Cāraṇas sang his glory and out of joy showered flowers on him, Oh king.

devanagari: शङ्खदुन्दुभयो नेदुर्दिवि देवप्रचोदिताः । जगुर्गन्धर्वपतयः तुंबुरुप्रमुखा नृप ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhadundubhayo nedurdivi devapracoditāḥjagurgandharvapatayaḥ tuṃburupramukhā nṛpa
translation: Commanded by the celestials, conchs and kettledrums were sounded in the sky and leaders of Gandharvas (celestial artistes) headed by Tumbaru participated in singing.

devanagari: ततोऽनुरक्तैः पशुपैः परिश्रितो । राजन् स्वगोष्ठं सबलोऽव्रजद्धरिः । तथाविधान्यस्य कृतानि गोपिका । गायन्त्य ईयुर्मुदिता हृदिस्पृशः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato'nuraktaiḥ paśupaiḥ pariśritorājan svagoṣṭhaṃ sabalo'vrajaddhariḥtathāvidhānyasya kṛtāni gopikāgāyantya īyurmuditā hṛdispṛśaḥ
translation:
Oh king! Surrounded by his affectionate cowherdfriends and accompanied by Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa proceeded to Gokula from that place. And young cowherd women, delighted at heart, returned to their respective homes singing all the while such miraculous exploits of Kṛṣṇa (like lifting up of Govardhana) who has captivated their hearts.
[back to top]
